description: This figure presents multi-scale evidence linking aluminum content to dislocation slip mode transition and work hardening in Fe–Mn–C–Al low-density steels. Panel (a) plots the parameter W_R (×10⁻⁴ m) versus Al content (wt.%), showing a linear decrease from approximately 29 at 0 Al to 10 at 6 Al, predicting a transition from wavy to planar slip. Corresponding TEM micrographs at ε=0.1 contrast the deformation substructures: (a1) for 3Al shows dislocation cells (DCs) characteristic of wavy slip, whereas (a2) for 6Al exhibits pronounced planar slip bands. Panel (b) provides an ECCI image at ε=0.14 revealing narrow, dense coplanar slip microbands. Panel (c) displays true stress and strain hardening rate versus true strain curves for the SRO-affected steel, illustrating the mechanical response and work hardening evolution. Collectively, the data demonstrate that increasing Al promotes planar dislocation glide, facilitates microband formation upon destruction of short-range order, and contributes to the dynamic Hall–Petch hardening and work hardening capacity in these steels.
